mappings.rationaleAI: Section B covers the nature and scope of engineering including its professional character, education, and branches.
Section B.2 lists civil engineering as one of the major branches of engineering.
Structural engineering is part of the broader engineering disciplines covered in B.2.
Section C covers the nature and scope of agricultural sciences including soil science, plant/animal production, and agricultural engineering.
Section D.1 covers bionics including mimicry of nature for engineering and natural models for engineering problems, connecting to robotics.
Section D.1.b mentions the human brain as a model for computers and information-processing devices, directly connecting to AI.
Nanotechnology is a recently developed interscience discipline (D) combining physics, chemistry, and engineering.
Materials science is fundamental to engineering (B) and connects to chemical and mechanical engineering branches.
Energy systems engineering connects to electrical (B.2) and mechanical engineering as branches of the technological sciences.
Renewable energy technology involves electrical, chemical, and mechanical engineering disciplines (B.2).
Section B.2 lists nuclear engineering as a branch of engineering.
The history of technological sciences (A) includes military technology and its relationship to engineering development.
Innovation is central to the technological sciences, driving the development of new technologies and methods.
Section D.1 references computer memory devices and information-processing devices, connecting to the bionics-computing interface.
Computer science underpins modern engineering including bionics (D.1) and systems engineering (D.2).
Section D.2 covers systems engineering and operations research, which involves network analysis and optimization.
Section D.2 covers systems engineering, which draws on complexity theory for analyzing complex technical systems.
Computing technology is part of the history of technological sciences (A) and connects to bionics and systems engineering (D).
Nuclear weapons technology relates to nuclear engineering (B.2) and the history of technological sciences (A).
Landscape architecture involves engineering and agricultural science principles applied to land design.
